channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8 (Fast Mode)
item.description: Jun 9, 03:42 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. Claude Opus 4.8 (Fast Mode) saw elevated errors from 7:50 PM PT / 02:50 UTC to 8:30 PM PT / 03:30 UTC. Jun 9, 03:13 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: A Single Scratch on Fiber Optics Was Scrapping Millions Each Year — Until One Engineer Rethought the Problem
item.description: An engineer at a US-listed optical communications manufacturer developed a three-category defect classification system for fiber coating scratches, saving 5-10 million RMB annually by challenging industry dogma.

item.title: DeepSeek Website Hits 541M Monthly Visits in May as Baidu's AI Products Stage a Comeback
item.description: DeepSeek recorded 541 million monthly visits in May, maintaining its #1 ranking in China, while Baidu's AI products across search, long-form content, and conversational AI showed strong recovery.

item.title: Tencent Lays Out Its Full AI Agent Strategy With WorkBuddy Enterprise and Agent Suite
item.description: Tencent has launched WorkBuddy Enterprise Edition with a three-layer 'Expert + Assistant + Team' model alongside the Agent Suite ecosystem, positioning itself as the unified enterprise AI workflow platform.

item.title: CATL Invests in DeepSeek: Zeng Yuqun's AI Energy Strategy Takes Shape
item.description: CATL has invested in DeepSeek's first funding round, marking the battery giant's strategic entry into AI as Zeng Yuqun pivots toward AI data center energy infrastructure with over $1 billion in AIDC investments.

item.title: West China Hospital Develops MicroSpine: A 2mm Robotic Arm System for Minimally Invasive Spinal Surgery
item.description: Sichuan University West China Hospital's MicroSpine surgical robot, featuring three 2mm robotic arms in an 8mm sheath, achieves posterior spinal target access with 100% accuracy in preclinical testing.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Mexico
item.description: Jun 9, 01:30 UTC Resolved - Connectivity errors (HTTP 500s) and elevated latency observed due to a hardware failure between 01:32 - 01:36 UTC 2026-06-09. Cloudflare has mitigated the issue.

item.title: Increased HTTP 500 errors for R2
item.description: Jun 9, 00:50 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 9, 00:09 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 23:43 UTC Investigating - Some Cloudflare R2 users may have seen increased errors when using R2

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 8, 22:36 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. Claude Opus 4.8 saw elevated errors from 3:06 PT / 22:06 UTC through 3:28 PT / 22:28 UTC. Jun 8, 22:20 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 8, 21:35 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 21:05 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
item.description: Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container. The flaw, CVE-2026-23111, sits in the kernel's nf_tables packet-filtering code and was patched upstream on February...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jun 8, 19:52 UTC Resolved - The incident is now resolved. The impact started at 11:35 PT / 18:35 UTC and ended at 12:30 PT / 19:30 UTC. Jun 8, 19:10 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: RealtimeKit performance issues
item.description: Jun 8, 18:23 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 8, 17:54 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 16:56 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 8, 16:34 UTC Investigating - Some RealtimeKit...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 8, 17:55 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 17:15 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated 500 errors on CF1 SaaS Integrations API
item.description: Jun 8, 17:51 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 17:36 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare One's SaaS integrations page has reported elevated HTTP 500 errors on API requests impacting ability to create, view, and manage integrations.

item.title: A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform
item.description: The company founded by Yossi Torati, Omer Gull, and Yuval Itzchakov has emerged from stealth mode. The post A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
item.description: Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group. In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users....

item.title: GraphQL elevated latency
item.description: Jun 8, 16:57 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 8, 16:14 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 8, 15:47 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue where some customers may experience latency querying the GraphQL API. We are...

item.title: INC20000030
item.description: Jun 8, 16:40 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've monitored the environment and confirmed that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in Snowsight.Customer experience: Customers hosted in...

item.title: Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team
item.description: AI-driven development is not something organizations can or should block. But it must be governed. The post Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
item.description: Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of a logic flow...

item.title: WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order
item.description: The Meta-owned communications app is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO. The post WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
item.description: Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine. Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
item.description: Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked. A chatbot got fooled. A bot token got leaked inside the malware. The same old mistakes showed up again. And...

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Akamai, Check Point, Cisco, Cyera, Dragos, WatchGuard and Zscaler. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites
item.description: The flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely and has been exploited in the wild for two months. The post Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: how the World Cup ball will fly and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far Much is new about this month’s FIFA World Cup tournament. It hosts more teams than ever before....

item.title: Building Pakistan Notice Helper: A Small AI Tool for a Very Local Safety Problem
Disruption with Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 8, 10:03 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 8, 09:49 UTC Update - We are experiencing degraded availability for the Claude Opus...

item.title: Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far
item.description: Much is new about this month’s upcoming FIFA World Cup tournament, which will be held in the US, Canada, and Mexico. It hosts more teams than ever before. It’s the first to occur in three different host countries. And, like predecessor cups for over half a century, it will employ a soccer ball with...

item.title: Pull Requests and Issues unavailable for signed-out users
item.description: Jun 8, 08:36 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 8, 08:35 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Actions, Issues and Pull Requests...

item.title: Detecting and Mitigating Bias by Treating Fairness as a Symmetry Operation
item.description: arXiv:2606.06514v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning systems deployed in high stakes socioeconomic settings routinely display bias. We formalize bias as a symmetry breaking operation: a classifier is fair if its outputs remain invariant under the counterfactual operation of switching a...

item.title: DiBS: Diffusion-Informed Branch Selection
item.description: arXiv:2606.06518v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sudoku is a representative constraint satisfaction problem that requires global structural reasoning under strict discrete constraints. The existing works of solving Sudoku mainly focus on two dominant approaches, i.e., traditional heuristic and deep...

item.title: SafeGene: Reusable Adapters for Transferable Safety Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2606.06519v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Open-weight LLMs are increasingly fine-tuned into customized assistants, but downstream fine-tuning can weaken safety alignment and make models more vulnerable to malicious prompts, even when the training data is not intentionally harmful. This...

item.title: Lean4Agent: Formal Modeling and Verification for Agent Workflow and Trajectory
item.description: arXiv:2606.06523v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Equipping Large Language Models (LLMs) to execute reliable multi-step workflows has become a central challenge in artificial intelligence. Despite recent advances in LLMs' agentic capabilities, most agent systems still lack formal methods for...

item.title: CrowdMath: A Dataset of Crowdsourced Mathematical Research Discussions
item.description: arXiv:2606.06526v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have made substantial progress on mathematical reasoning, but existing benchmarks typically evaluate well-specified problems with final answers, step-by-step solutions, or complete proofs. They do not capture collaborative...

item.title: Elmes*: Automated Construction of Fine-Grained Evaluation Rubrics for Large Language Models in Long-Tail Educational Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2606.06546v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating large language models (LLMs) for education requires measuring how models teach, not only what they know. Existing benchmarks emphasize domain-general correctness or depend on manually designed rubrics that scale poorly to long-tail...

item.title: FAIR-Calib: Frontier-Aware Instability-Reweighted Calibration for Post-Training Quantization of Diffusion Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.06547v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) refine tokens iteratively but commit them irreversibly, leading to a "stability lag" where early decisions remain fragile even after being written. We reveal that Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) error easily...

item.title: Multi-Scale Feature Attention Network for Polymer Classification using THz Dual-Comb Spectroscopy
item.description: arXiv:2606.06554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliable polymer identification is essential for ensuring the quality and safety of recycled plastics, yet conventional sorting and spectroscopic techniques often struggle to deliver robust discrimination. Terahertz Dual-Comb Spectroscopy (THz-DCS)...

item.title: MacArena: Benchmarking Computer Use Agents on an Online macOS Environment
item.description: arXiv:2606.06560v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computer-use agents (CUAs) operate graphical user interfaces (GUIs) through vision and control primitives, and their capabilities have advanced rapidly, driven in part by standardized online evaluation benchmarks such as OSWorld, which serve both as...

item.title: WAV: Multi-Resolution Block Residual Routing for Deep Decoder-Only Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2606.06564v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Residual connections are central to training deep Transformers, but standard PreNorm residual streams aggregate sublayer updates with fixed unit weights. Recent Attention Residuals replace this fixed accumulation with content-dependent depth-wise...

item.title: Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.06586v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained predominantly on English data encode substantial world knowledge, yet often fail to express it reliably in other languages, a phenomenon known as cross-lingual factual inconsistency. To study and address this, we...

item.title: Re-Centering Humans in LLM Personalization
item.description: arXiv:2606.06614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite growing interest, most evaluations of large language models' (LLMs') personalization abilities have relied on synthetic data. It remains unclear how well current personalization systems work for real users. In this paper, we study the gap in...

item.title: UnpredictaBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Distributional Randomness in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.06622v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce UnpredictaBench, an evaluation that tests the ability of large language models (LLMs) to capture true underlying distributions. As LLMs are increasingly used as substitutes for other entities (e.g., for humans in economic simulations),...

item.title: How Language Models Fail: Token-Level Signatures of Committed and Persistent Reasoning Failures
item.description: arXiv:2606.06635v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Failures in language model reasoning emerge through distinct processes that leave identifiable signatures in the reasoning trace. We characterize these failures using token-level uncertainty signals, finding they arise through two empirically...

item.title: CAF-Gen: A Multi-Agent System for Enriching Argumentation Structures
item.description: arXiv:2606.06646v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formalizing complex reasoning from natural text is one of the central challenges in computational linguistics. It requires systems to understand not just keywords but also the context and complex reasoning embedded in a text. Current Argument Mining...

item.title: The Open Source Community is backing OpenEnv for Agentic RL
Amazing Digital Dentures (a failed project)
Disruption in service availabilty for Go users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Disruption in service availabilty for Free and Go users
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified the issue for the listed services. We are currently working on a mitigation.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)
